# Transcrição: 9. Why's and APES

- **Fonte:** 9. Why's and APES
- **Duração:** 02:42
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:42]

Hey, why do people want any product? Look, for a couple of reasons. It makes them look good. Appearance. It makes them feel good. Performance and comfort. Makes sense. They save money maybe. It's economical. Or it makes them feel safe. I have safety and security so I'm going to do it. APES is an acronym for your easy memory. It's four major reasons people buy things. A. Appearance. P. Performance. E is economical. And S is security. Is it make me look good? Will I feel good? Does it somehow save me money or worry and will it somehow make us safer or more secure? Pick anything people buy. Any product. And you'll find out that these are their motives. Look, I've owned, I don't know how many phones. 20, 25, 30 different telephones. I bought a new phone because the new style was hot. Okay, that's appearance. I upgraded because they came out with a new version that was faster and smaller. That's performance. I bought another new phone because my texting was free. That was an economical decision. And I bought a new phone once because the last one I had was failing me. I dropped it in the toilet too many times. Okay, and what is that? Safety and security. Dependability. I need a new phone. The same with your clients. Find out why. What is the why? What is the APES? You will find it will be one of the A, the P, the E or the S with possibly one supporting the primary one. I had a client once spent $80,000 to buy a new car for me because he had a flat tire on his other car. His other car was one year old in perfect condition, but he decided to replace the old car with an $80,000 purchase and his justification was this. He needed a $1400 wheel and tire. He's like, I'm just not going to do it. I'm not going to spend $1400 on a car. I don't want any more. I'd rather spend $80,000 on one I do want. His whole decision was made and based on economics. Another man wants to buy a car from me because he had a date the night that he was taking delivery of the vehicle. He's like, I got to have it tonight, man. I got this date. What's his deal? Appearance, ego, am I going to look good? It's certainly not going to improve his performance, right? But it might give him an edge. He wanted to look good, feel good and believed it would improve his possibilities. Look, every day people spend money to acquire APES. The home I live in today, for instance, I made a decision on for one reason. I believed it was an economical thing to do and it was the most money I'd ever spent on any single one product. But in my mind, I made a decision that it was economical, smart, maybe even supported by safety and security that my money would be safe in that house. Learn the A, the P, the E and the S.
